description: Full group conversations feature — backend refactor, serialization, filtering, GroupContactInfo sidebar, group management (create/members/metadata/invite/join requests), group message bubbles, @mention system, real-time updates.

userStories.title: Rename conversation_type to group_type on conversations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to rename the conversation_type column on conversations to group_type to avoid collisions with the frontend routing conversationType concept.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration renames column conversation_type to group_type on the conversations table
Conversation model enum updated: enum group_type: { individual: 0, group: 1 }
All backend references updated: model, concerns (GroupConversationHandler), services (WhatsappBaileysService, baileys_handlers/*), controllers, specs, JBuilder serializers
Frontend references to conversation_type (routing concept with values mention/participating/unattended) remain untouched
bundle exec rspec passes with no failures
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Serialize group_type fields in API responses
userStories.description: As a frontend consumer, I need group_type in both conversation and contact JSON responses so the UI can distinguish groups from individual conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: group_type field added to app/views/api/v1/conversations/partials/_conversation.json.jbuilder (outputs 'individual' or 'group')
group_type field added to app/views/api/v1/models/_contact.json.jbuilder (outputs 'individual' or 'group')
Existing conversation and contact API responses include the new fields without regression
bundle exec rspec spec/controllers passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — group_type filter on conversations index
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the conversations index endpoint to accept a group_type query param so the basic filter dropdown can filter by conversation type.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/accounts/:account_id/conversations accepts group_type param with values 'individual' and 'group'
When group_type param is present, results are filtered by the group_type column
When group_type param is absent or 'all', no filter is applied (existing behavior unchanged)
bundle exec rspec spec/controllers/api/v1/accounts/conversations_controller_spec.rb passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — add group_type to filter_keys.yml and FilterService
userStories.description: As a developer, I need group_type supported in the advanced filter system so agents can build complex filters including conversation type.
acceptanceCriteria: group_type added to lib/filters/filter_keys.yml under conversations: with attribute_type: standard, data_type: text, filter_operators: [equal_to, not_equal_to]
FilterService correctly applies group_type filters against the conversations.group_type column
POST /api/v1/accounts/:account_id/conversations/filter with group_type filter returns only matching conversations
bundle exec rspec spec/services/filter_service_spec.rb passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — group creation endpoint
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to create a new WhatsApp group from Chatwoot so I can start group conversations directly.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/accounts/:account_id/groups accepts { inbox_id, subject, participants: [phone_numbers] }
Service delegates to channel provider (WhatsappBaileysService) which calls Baileys groupCreate(subject, participants)
On success: creates Contact (group_type: group), ContactInbox, Conversation (group_type: group), and ConversationGroupMember records for all participants
Returns the created conversation with group metadata
Returns 403 if agent does not have access to the inbox
Returns 422 with error message if provider is unavailable
bundle exec rspec spec/controllers/api/v1/accounts/groups_controller_spec.rb passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — add/remove members and role management endpoints
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to add/remove members from a group and promote/demote admins via the API.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/accounts/:account_id/contacts/:id/group_members accepts { participants: [phone_numbers] } and adds members via Baileys groupParticipantsUpdate with action add
DELETE /api/v1/accounts/:account_id/contacts/:id/group_members accepts { participants: [phone_numbers] } and removes members via Baileys groupParticipantsUpdate with action remove
PATCH /api/v1/accounts/:account_id/contacts/:id/group_members/:member_id accepts { role: 'admin' | 'member' } and calls Baileys groupParticipantsUpdate with action promote or demote
Backend updates ConversationGroupMember records accordingly (create, soft-delete via is_active, update role)
Returns 422 with error message if provider is unavailable
bundle exec rspec spec/controllers/api/v1/accounts/contacts/group_members_controller_spec.rb passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — group metadata update endpoint
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to edit group name and description via the API so agents can manage group metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: PATCH /api/v1/accounts/:account_id/contacts/:id/group_metadata accepts { subject, description }
Service calls Baileys groupUpdateSubject when subject is present
Service calls Baileys groupUpdateDescription when description is present
Backend updates Contact record: name from subject, additional_attributes.description from description
Avatar upload for group contacts uses existing contacts#update with avatar param, which calls Baileys updateProfilePicture
Returns 422 with error message if provider is unavailable
bundle exec rspec spec/controllers/api/v1/accounts/contacts/group_metadata_controller_spec.rb passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — invite link management endpoints
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to get and revoke the group invite link via the API.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/accounts/:account_id/contacts/:id/group_invite returns { invite_code, invite_url } by calling Baileys groupInviteCode(jid)
POST /api/v1/accounts/:account_id/contacts/:id/group_invite/revoke revokes the current link and returns a new { invite_code, invite_url } by calling Baileys groupRevokeInvite(jid)
Returns 422 with error message if provider is unavailable
bundle exec rspec spec/controllers/api/v1/accounts/contacts/group_invite_controller_spec.rb passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — join request management endpoints
userStories.description: As an agent/admin, I want to list and approve/reject pending join requests via the API.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/accounts/:account_id/contacts/:id/group_join_requests lists pending join requests by calling Baileys groupRequestParticipantsList(jid)

POST /api/v1/accounts/:account_id/contacts/:id/group_join_requests/handle accepts { participants: [jids], action: 'approve' | 'reject' } and calls Baileys groupRequestParticipantsUpdate(jid, participants, action)
Returns 422 with error message if provider is unavailable
bundle exec rspec spec/controllers/api/v1/accounts/contacts/group_join_requests_controller_spec.rb passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — extend MentionService for contact mentions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the backend MentionService to handle mention://contact/ID/Name URIs in group messages and create Mention records for mentioned contacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Messages::MentionService extracts mention://contact/ID/EncodedName URIs from message content
Creates Mention records (or equivalent) for each mentioned contact ID
Existing mention://user/ and mention://team/ handling is unchanged
bundle exec rspec spec/services/messages/mention_service_spec.rb passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend — API clients for all group endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need frontend API client methods for all group-related backend endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: New file app/javascript/dashboard/api/groupMembers.js exists

Exports: getGroupMembers(accountId, contactId), syncGroup(accountId, contactId), createGroup(accountId, params), updateGroupMetadata(accountId, contactId, params), addMembers(accountId, contactId, participants), removeMembers(accountId, contactId, participants), updateMemberRole(accountId, contactId, memberId, role), getInviteLink(accountId, contactId), revokeInviteLink(accountId, contactId), getPendingRequests(accountId, contactId), handleJoinRequest(accountId, contactId, params)
All methods follow existing ApiClient patterns from app/javascript/dashboard/api/contacts.js
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend — Vuex store module groupMembers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Vuex store module to manage group member state so components can reactively access and mutate group data.
acceptanceCriteria: New file app/javascript/dashboard/store/modules/groupMembers.js exists
State: records (object keyed by contactId → array of members), uiFlags: { isFetching, isSyncing, isUpdating }
Getter getGroupMembers(contactId) returns members array for the given contactId
Action fetch({ contactId }) calls getGroupMembers API and commits SET_GROUP_MEMBERS
Action sync({ contactId }) calls syncGroup API and then re-fetches via fetch
Actions addMembers, removeMembers, updateMemberRole call corresponding API methods and update store
Module registered in app/javascript/dashboard/store/index.js
pnpm test app/javascript/dashboard/store/modules/groupMembers.spec.js passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend — i18n keys for group features (en + pt-BR)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all group-related UI strings defined in i18n so no bare strings are used in components.
acceptanceCriteria: New or updated JSON file(s) in app/javascript/dashboard/i18n/locale/en/ with keys for: group info header, member list section title, admin badge label, sync button label, sync success message, sync error message, empty member list state, filter type label (All/Individual/Group), group creation modal labels, edit metadata labels (name/description/avatar), invite link section labels (copy/revoke), member management labels (add/remove/promote/demote), join request labels (approve/reject/pending count), mention dropdown header
All same keys added to app/javascript/dashboard/i18n/locale/pt_BR/ with Portuguese translations
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend — add group_type filter to ConversationBasicFilter
userStories.description: As an agent, I want a Type filter section in the basic filter dropdown to quickly show only individual or group conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: ConversationBasicFilter.vue has a new Type section in the dropdown, alongside Status and Sort Order
Options: All (default), Individual, Group — using i18n labels
Selected value is stored in UI settings under conversations_filter_by.group_type and restored on reload
Filter value is included in conversationFilters and passed as group_type query param to the fetch API call
Selecting Individual shows only conversations with group_type individual, selecting Group shows only group_type group, All shows all
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — add group_type to advanced filter system
userStories.description: As an agent, I want group_type available as a filter attribute in the advanced filter so I can build complex filter rules.
acceptanceCriteria: New entry in advancedFilterItems/index.js: attributeKey group_type, inputType multi_select, filterOperators OPERATOR_TYPES_1 (equal_to, not_equal_to), attributeModel standard
Added to filterAttributeGroups under Standard Filters
Filter options for group_type: individual and group with i18n labels
Advanced filter correctly sends group_type in filter payload and the backend returns matching conversations
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — GroupContactInfo basic display
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to see a group-specific info panel in the sidebar with the group avatar, name, member count, and member list with skeleton loading.
acceptanceCriteria: New component app/javascript/dashboard/routes/dashboard/conversation/contact/GroupContactInfo.vue using <script setup> and Composition API
Displays group avatar (using existing Avatar component), group name, and active member count
Member list shows each active member with: Avatar, name, Admin badge (only for role admin — no badge for member)
Skeleton loading placeholders appear while isFetching is true in the groupMembers store
Empty state message shown when fetch completes with zero members
On mount, dispatches groupMembers/fetch with the group contact ID
Only active members (is_active: true) are displayed
Tailwind only, no custom or scoped CSS
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — GroupContactInfo sync button
userStories.description: As an agent, I want a Sync button in GroupContactInfo to refresh the member list from the provider.
acceptanceCriteria: GroupContactInfo has a Sync button (using NextButton component) with sync/refresh icon
Clicking Sync dispatches groupMembers/sync action and shows spinner on the button while isSyncing is true
On sync success, shows a success alert using the existing useAlert composable
On sync error, shows an error alert with a user-friendly message
Button is disabled while sync is in progress
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — integrate GroupContactInfo in ContactPanel
userStories.description: As a system, I need ContactPanel to render GroupContactInfo instead of ContactInfo when the current conversation is a group conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: ContactPanel.vue imports GroupContactInfo
When currentChat.group_type === 'group', renders GroupContactInfo with the contact prop instead of ContactInfo
When currentChat.group_type is 'individual' or absent, renders existing ContactInfo (no regression)
Sidebar title changes: shows 'Group' (i18n key) for group conversations, existing 'Contact' for individual
contact_notes and contact_attributes accordion sections remain visible for group conversations
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — group creation UI modal
userStories.description: As an agent, I want a modal to create a new WhatsApp group by selecting an inbox, entering a group name, and choosing initial participants.
acceptanceCriteria: New component for group creation modal accessible from a relevant UI entry point
Inbox selector shows only inboxes that support groups (Baileys/WhatsApp channel type)
Group name text input field (required)
Participant selector with search by contact name or phone number
Submit calls createGroup API (via store action), shows loading state on the submit button
On success: shows success notification and navigates to the new group conversation
On error: shows error notification with the error message
Tailwind only, <script setup> and Composition API
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — member management UI in GroupContactInfo
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to add/remove members and change roles directly from the GroupContactInfo sidebar.
acceptanceCriteria: Add member button in GroupContactInfo opens a contact search/select input
Selecting a contact and confirming calls addMembers store action; member appears in list on success
Each member row has a context menu or action buttons: Promote to admin (if member), Demote to member (if admin), Remove from group
Each action calls the corresponding store action and shows loading state on the row
Success and error alerts shown after each action
Tailwind only, <script setup> and Composition API
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — group metadata editing UI
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to edit the group name, description, and avatar inline from the GroupContactInfo sidebar.
acceptanceCriteria: Group name is inline-editable: click to activate edit input, press Enter or blur to save
Description field is editable: click to activate textarea, blur to save
Avatar element is clickable and opens a file picker; selected image is uploaded via existing contact avatar update flow
Each save calls updateGroupMetadata API (name/description) or contacts#update (avatar)
Loading state on the field during save; success and error alerts shown
Tailwind only, <script setup> and Composition API
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — invite link management UI
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to view, copy, and revoke the group invite link from the sidebar.
acceptanceCriteria: Invite Link section in GroupContactInfo is always visible (not collapsed)
On section mount, fetches the invite link via getInviteLink API and displays it
Copy button copies the invite URL to the clipboard and shows a brief confirmation
Revoke & Regenerate button calls revokeInviteLink API, shows loading state, and displays the new link on success
Error alert shown if fetch or revoke fails
Tailwind only, <script setup> and Composition API
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — join request management UI
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to see and act on pending join requests in the sidebar when join approval is enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: Pending Requests section in GroupContactInfo is only rendered when there are pending requests
On mount, fetches pending requests via getPendingRequests API
Each request shows requester name/phone with Approve and Reject buttons
Approve button calls handleJoinRequest with action approve; Reject with action reject
Handled requests are removed from the list; success/error alerts shown
Section disappears when list becomes empty
Tailwind only, <script setup> and Composition API
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — group message bubbles: sender name with color
userStories.description: As an agent, I want group message bubbles to show the sender's name above the text with a deterministic color per sender.
acceptanceCriteria: In group conversations (group_type === 'group'), incoming message bubbles display the sender's name above the message content
Sender name color is deterministic: index = sender.name.length % 6, color taken from the same AVATAR_COLORS palette used by the Avatar component
Sender name is NOT shown for consecutive messages from the same sender within the same render block
Individual conversation bubbles are unchanged (no sender name shown)
Implementation in components-next/ message bubble components
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — group message bubbles: sender avatar
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to see the sender's avatar next to group message bubbles to visually identify who sent each message.
acceptanceCriteria: In group conversations, each incoming message shows the sender's avatar to the left of the bubble
Avatar uses sender's thumbnail if available; otherwise shows initials placeholder with the same name-length color from US-024
Avatar is NOT repeated for consecutive messages from the same sender (show only on the first of a group)
Individual conversation bubbles unchanged
Implementation in components-next/ message bubble components
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — @mention dropdown for group conversations
userStories.description: As an agent, I want typing @ in a group conversation to show a dropdown of group members so I can mention them in messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Typing @ in the message editor for a group conversation (group_type === 'group') triggers a mention suggestion dropdown
Dropdown lists group members from the groupMembers store for the current conversation's group contact
Dropdown filters by the text typed after @ (matches on name or phone number)
Each dropdown item shows Avatar, name, and phone number
Selecting an item inserts a mention node: [@DisplayName](mention://contact/ID/EncodedName) into the ProseMirror editor
In individual conversations (or private notes), the existing agent mention behavior is unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — mention rendering in group message bubbles
userStories.description: As an agent, I want @mention tags in group messages to be visually highlighted in the message bubble.
acceptanceCriteria: mention://contact/ID/EncodedName URIs in message content are rendered as highlighted/styled mention chips in the bubble
Mention chip shows @DisplayName with a distinct background or text color
The markdown-it link plugin (or equivalent renderer) handles the mention://contact scheme
Existing mention://user and mention://team rendering is unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — ActionCable handler for contact.group_synced event
userStories.description: As a system, when a contact.group_synced event arrives via ActionCable, the group member list should update reactively without a page refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: ActionCable message handler listens for events with type contact.group_synced
On receiving the event, the handler extracts group member data directly from the event payload (no additional API call)
Commits the data to the groupMembers store using SET_GROUP_MEMBERS mutation for the affected contactId
GroupContactInfo member list updates reactively in the sidebar
pnpm test passes for the handler file
Typecheck passes
